seo.title: Salted Meat - for Christmas - Marinda Kook

html:
To have salted meat in your fridge is to have food in your house. It's tastier than any bought cold meat and there's a feeling of satisfaction inside you. Salted meat with mustard and lettuce on a sandwich just calls for a cold glass alongside.

 

YOU NEED:

  • salted meat / corned beef – available at supermarkets or at Fruit and Veg
  • 2 bay leaves
  • 5 whole cloves
  • 5 whole peppercorns
  • 1 onion sliced into rings
  • 3 carrots sliced into rounds
  • 1 sprig parsley
  • 15 ml brown sugar
  • 10 ml prepared mustard or 5 ml mustard powder
  • enough water to cover the meat in the pot

NOW:

  • slice the onion and the carrot into a pot
  • put the bay leaves, parsley, cloves, peppercorns, sugar and mustard on top of the onions and carrots
  • put the meat in the pot and cover it with water
  • now for every 500g the meat weighs, cook it for 30 minutes on the stove ( my meat weighed 1,5 kg and I cooked it slowly for 90 minutes)
  • take the meat out of the water and let it cool outside the fridge
  • now wrap the meat in cling wrap and store it in a plastic container in your fridge – it can really keep for a very long time in your fridge

Enjoy this bit of traditional meat on bread or in a salad. Mustard and sweet chilli sauce go beautifully with the salted meat.
